title: Monotone plus interaction-constrained XGBoost
summary: XGBoost documents that monotonic and interaction constraints can improve predictive performance when domain priors are strong.
rationale: XGBoost documents that monotonic and interaction constraints can improve predictive performance when domain priors are strong. Pit likelihood should usually rise with tyre age/degradation and late-race pressure within a regime, while many cross-feature interactions in unrestricted trees are probably spurious.
implementation_hint: Build a constrained XGB variant with monotone constraints on a small trusted subset such as TyreLife, Cumulative_Degradation, and late-race remaining-laps proxies, then restrict interactions so tyre-state features mainly interact with compound/race context and local position features. Increase max_bin because XGBoost notes constrained hist trees can become too shallow otherwise.
expected_effect: Better generalization across races and years; most likely gain is improved LB robustness rather than a big CV jump.
risk: Wrong-sign constraints can suppress real wet-race or edge-case behavior, and over-tight interaction blocks can underfit.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
